title: thelypteris indrapurae
intro: Thelypteris indrapurae, also known as the Indrapura fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a type of fern that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. Thelypteris indrapurae contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory infections.
